questions.question: What does the fr unit represent in CSS Grid?
choices: A fraction of the available space in the grid container
questions.explanation: The fr (fraction) unit represents a fraction of the available space in the grid container. For example, 1fr 2fr splits available space into thirds: one-third and two-thirds.

questions.question: Which CSS property defines the column structure of a grid?
questions.explanation: grid-template-columns defines the number and sizes of columns in a grid container. Values can use px, %, fr, or functions like repeat() and minmax().

questions.question: What does repeat(3, 1fr) produce in a grid-template-columns declaration?
choices: Three columns each taking one-third of the available space
questions.explanation: repeat(3, 1fr) is shorthand for 1fr 1fr 1fr, creating three columns that each take an equal fraction of the available space.

questions.question: How do you make a grid item span from column line 1 to column line 3?
questions.explanation: grid-column: 1 / 3 tells the item to start at column line 1 and end at column line 3, spanning two columns. The slash separates start and end line numbers.

questions.question: The ___ property sets the spacing between grid rows and columns.
questions.explanation: grid-gap (or its modern shorthand gap) sets the gutter size between grid rows and columns. It accepts one value for both or two values for row-gap and column-gap separately.

questions.question: The ___ property assigns a grid item to a named area defined by grid-template-areas.
questions.explanation: The grid-area property places a grid item into a named area that was defined on the container using grid-template-areas, or can be used as shorthand for grid-row and grid-column.
